headings.rules: Use for all primary page titles, hype scores, and major data callouts. Apply tracking-tight for large headings. DO NOT use Inter or generic sans.

body.rules: Use for data tables, metrics, percentages, and UI text. Enforces the 'technical' and terminal-like feel of a crypto dashboard.

labels.rules: Uppercase, tracking-[0.2em], text-xs. Used for overlines, table headers, and tiny alerts.

background.rules: Never use pure #000000 without slight texture. Backgrounds should feel deep and empty.

accent.rules: Use neon green for Pump Probability and positive changes. Use hot pink for Dump Risk/Fake Hype alerts. Use cyber yellow for trending/hype highlights. DO NOT use teal (#1B93A4) anywhere.

visual_enhancers.surfaces: GRID BORDERS & RETRO-FUTURISM. Expose the skeleton. Dashboards should use border-collapse style layouts with 1px solid borders (border-white/10) separating grid items. No soft drop shadows.
visual_enhancers.glow: Use drop-shadow-[0_0_8px_rgba(0,255,102,0.5)] for high-signal alerts (Pump probability > 80%).
visual_enhancers.glassmorphism: Only for sticky headers/navs: bg-black/60, backdrop-blur-xl, border-b border-white/10.

components.cards: Go cardless or sharp edges (max 4px radius). Focus on data density. Use 1px subtle borders (border-white/10) with flat backgrounds. Hover states should slightly lighten background and brighten the border.
components.buttons: Sharp pill-shaped or strictly rectangular. Primary actions should use High-Contrast Black text on Neon Accent background.
components.badges: Used for 'Hype Status' or 'Alerts'. Sharp edges, transparent backgrounds with 1px solid neon borders and neon text (e.g., border-[#FF007F] text-[#FF007F]).
components.tables: Dense. Use Shadcn table but override padding (py-2 px-4) for higher density. Align metrics correctly (right-align numbers).
components.charts: Use Chart.js. Lines must be solid neon colors with transparent fills (no thick gradients below the line). Grid lines must be barely visible (white/5).

grids_and_layouts.dashboard_grid: MODE B: High Density Grid. grid-cols-1 md:grid-cols-3 lg:grid-cols-4. Tight gaps (gap-4). Every pixel must be utilized.
grids_and_layouts.spacing: Generous Spacing Philosophy. Use p-6 or p-8 inside standalone containers. Containers with borders MUST have internal padding.

motion.rules: Minimalist and precise. Micro-interactions for feedback. Hovering a coin row should gently highlight it (bg-white/5). Alerts should pulse subtly.
motion.transitions: Use specific properties: transition: background-color 0.2s ease, border-color 0.2s ease.

features_design_mapping.trending_coins_list: Use dense data tables with JetBrains Mono. Metrics like +45% should use neon green text with a 10% opacity green background badge.
features_design_mapping.hype_score: Visual indicator using a highly saturated circular progress or progress bar (Chart.js doughnut or raw CSS). Cyber Yellow for high hype.
features_design_mapping.sentiment_meter: Horizontal gauge chart with sharp edges. Red (Dump/Fear) to Green (Pump/Greed) gradient, but constructed with segmented solid blocks, not a smooth gradient (retro-tech look).
features_design_mapping.mention_graphs: Chart.js line charts. The line should glow (use canvas drop shadow or shadow-like effects in Chart.js), no fill under the line.
features_design_mapping.alert_system: Use Sonner toasts with sharp 1px borders. Hot pink border for High Risk/Fake Hype alerts. These should pulse when entering.
features_design_mapping.pump_dump_probabilities: Display as large, mono-spaced typography elements (e.g., '87.4%') rather than just bars, to emphasize the quantitative edge.

accessibility.contrast: Ensure Normal Text (below 18px) meets minimum 4.5:1 ratio. Text colors MUST be explicitly set on hover.
accessibility.testing: All interactive elements MUST include data-testid (e.g., data-testid='coin-row-pepe', data-testid='alert-dismiss').
accessibility.reduced_motion: Respect prefers-reduced-motion for the pulse animations.

media.icons: Use Phosphor Icons (dense dashboard style). `yarn add @phosphor-icons/react`.
media.images: Use abstract neon backgrounds sparingly for empty states or the auth/landing page. No generic vectors.

image_urls.description: Close-up of a neon blue line chart on a grid, symbolizing data trends.
Abstract neon lines forming a digital landscape

3d_and_creative.implementation: Do not use D3 or Three.js unless required for a specific 'Hype Network' graph. Stick to Chart.js as mandated by the user, customized to look Electric & Neon.

instructions_to_main_agent: DO NOT use Inter or standard sans-serif for headings. Stick to Unbounded and JetBrains Mono.
DO NOT use Teal (#1B93A4) anywhere. It is forbidden.
DO NOT apply universal transition (transition: all). Use specific properties.
DO NOT use complete transparent backgrounds for the sticky header or floating overlays.
DO NOT center align the app container (.App { text-align: center; } is banned).
DO NOT make generic centered layouts. Use Mode B High Density grids.
Implement Chart.js visualizations clearly, stripping out default gray tooltips and replacing them with sharp, dark neon-bordered tooltips.
UNIVERSAL GUIDELINES FOR MAIN AGENT: You tend to converge toward generic, 'on distribution' outputs. In frontend design, this creates what users call the 'AI slop' aesthetic. Avoid this: make creative, distinctive frontends that surprise and delight.
Dark colors look good when used independently without gradients. DO NOT use dark colors as gradients.
Create depth through layered design elements with z-index hierarchy and 1px sharp borders.
Every interaction needs micro-animations - hover states, transitions, entrance animations. Static = dead.
NEVER use AI assistant Emoji characters (🤖🧠💭💡🔮🎯📚🎭🎬🎪🎉🎊🎁🎀🎂🍰🎈🎨🎰💰💵💳🏦💎🪙💸🤑📊📈📉💱🔢🏆🥇) for icons. Use Phosphor Icons.
Components MUST use named exports, Pages MUST use default exports.
All interactive and key informational elements MUST include a data-testid attribute.
Use sonner for toasts, located in /app/src/components/ui/sonner.tsx.
